claim: WOD official repo lists End-To-End Driving as one of three Waymo Open Dataset datasets, with camera data and high-level commands.
notes: evidence_class=official_repo_code; retrieved public README

claim: E2E v1.0.0 release notes record 2037 training runs, 479 validation runs with rater feedback labels/scenario tags, and 1505 testing runs.
notes: evidence_class=official_repo_code; official release notes

claim: Waymo terms grant non-commercial use for research, teaching, scientific publication, personal experimentation, and publication benchmarking, with production/vehicle-use restrictions.
notes: evidence_class=official_doc; access/licence phase should still review full legal terms

claim: Official access flow requires Google account login and agreement to Waymo Dataset License; E2E data/download pages redirect to Google sign-in.
notes: evidence_class=official_doc; no account created and no licence accepted

claim: E2EDFrame public release schema populates frame.context.name, camera_calibrations, frame.timestamp_micros, and frame.images; all other Frame/context fields are unused.
notes: evidence_class=release_schema; B1-load-bearing for actor-track absence

claim: E2EDFrame includes ego future log states at 4 Hz, ego past history states at 4 Hz, and ego intent enum.
notes: evidence_class=release_schema

claim: Released preference_trajectories are repeated EgoTrajectoryStates with up to three human-scored trajectories on a subset of frames; invalid scores are -1 or empty and valid score range is 0-10.
notes: evidence_class=release_schema; metadata only; no rating values read

claim: Submission proto keys predictions by frame_name matching E2EDFrame.frame.context.name and requires a 5 s 4 Hz x/y trajectory with 20 waypoints.
notes: evidence_class=release_schema; supports candidate/prediction identity metadata

claim: Direct WOD-E2E public schema does not expose surrounding-actor 3D tracks, IDs, classes, histories, or uncertainty; only listed populated Frame fields are camera/calibration/timestamp/images and other Frame fields are unused.
notes: evidence_class=release_schema; Phase 4 should not infer tracks from paper prose

claim: Official public sources found no E2E-to-WOMD/WOD crosswalk; README distinguishes Motion object trajectories/3D maps from E2E camera/high-level-command dataset, but no mapping key is documented.
notes: evidence_class=official_repo_code/release_schema; absence based on public official sources, signed-in manifests remain unchecked

claim: Ego trajectory coordinates are meters in a right-handed vehicle frame with +x forward, +y left, +z up, origin at middle of ego rear axle.
notes: evidence_class=release_schema; note paper prose differs on origin

claim: Total compressed and expanded WOD-E2E release size is unverified from public official sources because data/download pages are sign-in gated and no public official size manifest was found.
notes: evidence_class=official_doc for blocked access; confidence medium for absence from public sources

claim: Waymo Dataset License Agreement for Non-Commercial Use provides a non-commercial research and scientific-publication path compatible with RQ010A/RQ010B research if restrictions are followed.
notes: evidence_class=official_doc; no license accepted by worker

claim: Waymo license restricts redistribution: small illustrative extracts may be published; dataset copies/modifications are limited to registered licensees; WOD-derived models/associated derivatives require downstream non-commercial terms and attribution.
notes: evidence_class=official_doc; derived track release should be license-reviewed

claim: Waymo license forbids using or deploying the dataset or WOD-trained/refined models in vehicle operation, production systems, or primarily commercial purposes.
notes: evidence_class=official_doc; RQ010 must remain research-only

claim: Access requires Google sign-in and agreement to the Waymo Dataset License; public E2E data/download pages are sign-in gated.

claim: Public official sources do not verify exact WOD-E2E GCS bucket URI, shard counts, checksums, official compressed size, or signed-in regional/per-dataset opt-in constraints.

claim: The public E2EDFrame schema populates camera/calibration/timestamp/image fields plus ego and preference trajectory fields, and does not expose direct surrounding-actor tracks or map geometry.
notes: evidence_class=release_schema; supports Route 1 infeasible_now and T2 route prerequisites

claim: Official Waymo README distinguishes Perception, Motion, and End-To-End Driving: Motion has object trajectories and 3D maps, while E2E has camera data and high-level commands; no crosswalk is stated there.
notes: evidence_class=official_repo_code; supports Route 2/3 as hypothesis rather than viable

claim: The official E2E challenge page states WOD-E2E has 4021 20-second run segments, training/validation full sequence and pose information, test first 12 seconds, and 8-camera 360-degree input.
notes: evidence_class=official_doc; supports Route 4/5 data prerequisites and Phase 7 scale handoff

claim: Waymo terms allow non-commercial research/scientific-publication use but restrict sharing and production/vehicle use of the dataset and derivatives.
notes: evidence_class=official_doc; applies to all route license constraints

claim: MUTR3D is an online multi-camera 3D tracking framework using known camera transformations and reports a +5.3 AMOTA improvement on nuScenes.
notes: evidence_class=primary_paper; benchmark proxy only, not WOD-E2E field availability or guaranteed accuracy

claim: Sparse4D v3 reports nuScenes benchmark tracking performance of 49.0% AMOTA with ResNet-50 and 67.7% AMOTA for its best model.
notes: evidence_class=primary_paper; benchmark proxy only, no WOD-E2E guarantee

claim: CC-3DT performs panoramic cross-camera 3D object tracking and reports a 12.6% AMOTA improvement among camera-based methods on the nuScenes 3D tracking benchmark.
notes: evidence_class=primary_paper; benchmark proxy only, no WOD-E2E guarantee

claim: MapTR is an online vectorized HD map construction method using camera input and reports real-time MapTR-nano inference at 25.1 FPS on RTX 3090 on nuScenes.
notes: evidence_class=primary_paper; supports inferred map/lane fallback only, not official WOD-E2E map availability

claim: LET-3D-AP paper identifies camera-only 3D detection depth/longitudinal localization error as a central evaluation issue, motivating depth-tolerant QA for camera-only tracking routes.
notes: evidence_class=primary_paper; supports expected failure modes and QA focus for Route 4/5

claim: Primary protocol is a shared open-loop opportunity structure: one frozen actor forecast/history object is produced from pre-critical tracked actor history and shared across all ego candidates.
notes: Compares candidates under the same opportunity structure; does not claim closed-loop actor response or realised harm

claim: Sensitivity protocol is candidate-conditioned actor forecasting with explicit uncertainty, model provenance, and outcome-blind rating-free training; predicted responses are not realised harm.
notes: Sensitivity only after primary protocol; no rating-tuned predictor allowed

claim: Critical-frame alignment table records candidate horizon, sampling frequency, first candidate waypoint anchor, actor-history cutoff, shared forecast horizon, coordinate-transform chain, and degradation under unverified original-run index or missing map/route reference.
source_or_path: 01_results/exports/candidate_future_actor_protocol.md; field_availability_crosswalk.csv rows REQ_CANDIDATE_HORIZON_SAMPLING, REQ_CANDIDATE_FIRST_STATE_ANCHOR, REQ_SCENE_CRITICAL_FRAME_INDEX, REQ_SCENE_MAP_CONFLICT_GEOMETRY
notes: N4 acceptance item; unresolved original-run index or unvalidated transforms force abstention

claim: Leak-safety boundary is ratings_read_allowed: false; counterpart selection must be rating-independent and scenes abstain when counterpart tracking, history, occlusion, transforms, map requirements, or forecast support are insufficient.
notes: Blocks rating values, score ranks, rating-tuned predictors, post-critical actor observations, and silent M2 substitution

claim: Recommended RQ010B default is the shared open-loop protocol; escalate to candidate-conditioned sensitivity only for pre-registered robustness with rating-free model provenance and uncertainty.
notes: Supports honest candidate comparison while preserving open-loop and no-leakage boundaries

claim: CLEAR/MOTA-style tracking conventions combine false positives, missed targets, and identity switches; ID switches are counted from matched track identities.
notes: Metric convention only; not WOD-E2E field availability or a WOD-E2E accuracy guarantee

claim: IDF1/identity metric conventions compute IDR, IDP, and IDF1 from IDTP, IDFN, and IDFP after global identity assignment.
notes: Metric convention only; used to define report-only identity comparability

claim: AV tracking benchmarks commonly match predicted and reference objects by 2D center distance on the ground plane; nuScenes tracking uses a 2 m center-distance threshold.
notes: Convention proxy for Route 4/5 QA matching; not a WOD-E2E validation result

claim: AV detection/tracking result formats conventionally express bounding-box translation in meters and velocity as vx/vy in m/s; nuScenes defines ATE as 2D Euclidean center distance and AVE as absolute velocity error.
notes: Supports position and velocity error definitions only

claim: HOTA is a standard MOT metric that balances detection, association, and localization; TrackEval implements HOTA from DetA and AssA with localization submetrics.
notes: Report-only comparability metric; hard M3 gates remain state and critical-frame specific

claim: Uncertainty-aware MOT evaluation can use negative log likelihood of the tracker posterior against ground truth; this explicitly scores uncertainty estimates rather than only detections and associations.
notes: Supports uncertainty-calibration gate vocabulary; no WOD-E2E uncertainty performance is claimed

claim: Phase 7 compute/storage estimates use Phase 3 derived size bounds: full logical compressed WOD-E2E about 0.15-17 TB and minimal logical three-run pilot about 0.1-11 GB; official manifest sizes remain sign-in gated.
notes: evidence_class=derived_estimate; load-bearing as uncertainty bound only, not official size

claim: TensorFlow TFRecordDataset reads records as bytes and does not parse or decode them on its own; parsing/decoding occurs in later map transforms, and buffer_size plus num_parallel_reads are relevant I/O controls.
notes: evidence_class=official_doc; supports TFRecord decode/I/O assumptions, not a WOD-E2E throughput benchmark

claim: TensorFlow's tf.data performance guide warns that remote storage can have higher time-to-first-byte and single-file throughput limits, and recommends prefetch/interleave to overlap and parallelize data loading.
notes: evidence_class=official_doc; supports storage/I/O uncertainty and need for Phase 8 throughput measurement

claim: StreamPETR is an online multi-view 3D detection proxy reporting a lightweight model at 31.7 FPS and 65.3% AMOTA on nuScenes; this is not a WOD-E2E benchmark.
notes: evidence_class=primary_paper benchmark_proxy; used only to bound GPU throughput plausibility

claim: MapTR-nano is a camera-only online vectorized HD map construction proxy reporting 25.1 FPS on RTX 3090 on nuScenes; this does not verify official WOD-E2E map availability.
notes: evidence_class=primary_paper benchmark_proxy; supports optional inferred-map cost bounds only

claim: MUTR3D is a multi-camera 3D tracking proxy that uses known camera transformations and reports a 5.3 AMOTA improvement on nuScenes; this is not WOD-E2E throughput or accuracy evidence.
notes: evidence_class=primary_paper benchmark_proxy; supports Route 4 feasibility pending pilot

claim: Sparse4D v3 is a multi-view detection/tracking proxy reporting 49.0% AMOTA with ResNet-50 and 67.7% AMOTA for the best nuScenes test model; this is not a WOD-E2E benchmark.
notes: evidence_class=primary_paper benchmark_proxy; supports Route 4 feasibility pending pilot

claim: Google Cloud Local SSD documentation gives storage capacity and throughput envelopes for attached local SSDs, including 375 GiB per Local SSD disk and multi-disk read/write throughput tables.
notes: evidence_class=official_doc; infrastructure envelope only, not project-cluster verification

claim: NVIDIA hardware documentation verifies representative GPU memory envelopes: RTX 4090 has 24 GB, RTX A6000 has 48 GB, and DGX H100/H200 systems provide an 8-GPU HPC-class node envelope with local NVMe data cache.
notes: evidence_class=official_doc; hardware envelope only, actual local/HPC availability unverified

claim: FIX B3 current selected-route recommendation is BLOCKED_PENDING_ACCESS because verified WOD-E2E scale and a WOD-E2E Route 4 benchmark are unavailable.
